Due to physiological reasons, data is more accurate for users between ages %1$d and %2$d. For other users, data should be used for reference only.\n\nOnly display weight and BMI?

People younger than %1$d, tend to have faster metabolisms and are still growing, so a lot of important indicators are constantly changing, making measurements less accurate. People older than %2$d usually start to experience bone loss, which also affects data accuracy. Therefore users in these categories should use this data for reference only.

Body composition data for individuals under %1$d years and over %2$d years will differ from the rest of the population. For these individuals, measurements other than weight and BMI should be used for reference only.

An individual\'s ideal weight is calculated on the basis of a combination of large samples of statistical data and that particular individual\'s unique physical characteristics.
Ideal body fat percentage refers to your optimum body fat ratio, and is based on the analysis of an individual\'s physical features in comparison to a large population of statistical data.

Stress is a physiological or psychological response to environmental pressures. Appropriate levels of stress can be beneficial to work or academic performance, but too much stress has a negative impact on physical and mental health.

Stress is a feeling of emotional or physical tension. It can come from events that make you feel frustrated, angry, or nervous. Moderate levels of stress can be beneficial and may improve your work efficiency. However, excessively high levels of stress can have a negative impact on your health.

Your stress level is controlled by your autonomic nervous system. High stress levels are associated with increased activity in the sympathetic nervous system, while lower levels are associated with activity in the parasympathetic nervous system. The state of your autonomic nervous system is reflected by fluctuations in your heart rate. This provides a means to measure stress.

Note: Stress test results may be affected by caffeine, nicotine, alcohol, and drug intake. Other factors such as heart disease, asthma, physical activity, and the application of physical pressure on the body may also affect test results.
Perform calibration when using this feature for the first time for more accurate results. Calibration includes a survey and heart rate check.
Enable automatic stress testing if your wearable device supports this feature. Your device will periodically measure your heart rate and evaluate your stress level.
This feature will provide analysis and suggestions based on your stress history.

Note: As your heart rate will rise during exercise, data collected during periods of increased physical activity will not be included in stress level calculations. Instead, we will use your resting heart rate as a basis for calculating stress.

* This technology provides stress level evaluations based on heart rate fluctuation data and stress models. It is certified by the Institute of Psychology, Chinese Academy of Sciences.\n* Results and suggestions are for reference only.

This scale can categorize users into 9 body types, taking body fat percentage, skeletal muscle mass, and other indicators into consideration. A person\'s body type can be used to broadly describe their physical appearance.
Read device identifier, name, status, and more.
Waist-to-hip ratio is calculated as the circumference of your waist divided by the circumference of your hips.\nNote: This number cannot be changed again once saved.
Including read/write data to the wearable through the Health app. Data includes any app files and content.

Note:\n%1$d. Data from third-party apps will not count towards Huawei Health activities, medals, or achievements.\n%2$d. The settings above are added for data security and can be used to deny the access to certain data.
Body weight is the total sum of a person\'s mass when they aren\'t wearing any clothes and are on an empty stomach.
BMI (body mass index) = weight (kg) / height (m)²\nBMI is an internationally used indicator for evaluating a person\'s body fat level and state of health.

Fat is a connective tissue composed of a large number of fat cells. Its functions include promoting the absorption of fat-soluble vitamins, protecting internal organs, regulating body temperature, and storing energy. Body fat percentage indicates the relative amount of fat in a person\'s body, and is an objective indicator of fat levels. Having an overly high or low body fat percentage can affect one\'s health.
Fat-free weight refers to a person\'s body weight minus the weight of their fat. It indicates the total weight of non-fat components in the body.

Visceral fat is the fat surrounding the abdominal organs. Excessive visceral fat can lead to a large belly, and increases the risk of cardiovascular disease. However, it is important to have an appropriate amount since visceral fat supports, stabilizes, and protects the abdominal organs.

Muscles are tissues that play an important role in producing energy, and managing body temperature, body movement, and the heartbeat. Muscle mass includes skeletal muscle, smooth muscle (such as internal organs), and water content. The amount of muscle in a body varies based on sex, age, and physical constitution.

The weight of skeletal muscle in the body. Skeletal muscle is attached to the skeleton and generates the force needed for physical movement. Muscle volume and weight directly affect physical strength and ability, and also play an important role in regulating metabolism.
The total weight of skeletal muscle in the limbs divided by the square of your body height should be above %1$s kg/m² for men and above %2$s kg/m² for women, or there is a risk of sarcopenia.

Body water percentage is the percentage of total body mass that is made up of water. The water inside a person\'s body has many important functions, including transporting nutrients, removing waste, and maintaining body temperature.
The amount of protein in the body. Protein is a vital component of the cells and tissues that make up the human body.

Bone mineral content refers to the amount of inorganic mineral in the bones. Bones are composed of inorganic mineral, organic matter, and bone cells. There is a significant correlation between low bone mineral content and osteoporosis.

Basal metabolic rate is the rate at which the body converts food into energy while awake and at rest, without considering external factors such as ambient temperature and stress. Generally, men have higher metabolisms than women, and young people higher metabolisms than the elderly.

Heart rate is the number of heart beats per minute. The typical heart rate of a person at rest is %1$d–%2$d bpm. Heart rate may vary between individuals due to differences in age, sex, or other physiological factors. The heart rate of athletes is typically around %3$d bpm, slower than that of the average adult.
Physical age reflects a person\'s health relative to the general population based on fat, skeletal muscle, and other indicators. A youthful physical age indicates better general health.

Body shape describes the distribution of fat in the body. Fruit names are used to make this easier to visualize. Banana-shaped bodies have uniform fat distribution, apple-shaped bodies have fat concentrated in the abdomen, and pear-shaped bodies have fat concentrated in the thighs. Apple-shaped bodies are at a higher risk of central obesity or hidden obesity.
Waist-to-hip ratio is the ratio of waist circumference to hip circumference. It should be less than %1$s for men and less than %2$s for women, or there is a risk of central obesity.
Apple-shape types are more prone to insulin resistance and developing diabetes, high blood pressure, and other conditions.
Increase aerobic exercise to help burn fat and get rid of visceral fat quicker.
Work out to fill out your upper body for a more balanced figure. Focus on your core and back muscles.
Keep working out to maintain your figure.
Get in aerobic exercise regularly to maintain your figure.
Keep working out to maintain your figure.

Blood sugar is above %.1f mmol/L, you are at risk of ketoacidosis (DKA). If you feel nauseous, start vomiting, or your breath tastes sour, you might want to consider a ketones urine test. If ketosis occurs, temporarily stop exercising and seek medical attention.

Blood sugar is above %.1f mmol/L, you are at risk of entering hyperosmolar hyperglycemic state (HHS). If you also test positive for ketone bodies, it\'s important to temporarily stop exercising. If you start feeling nauseous, thirsty, dehydrated, or drowsy, your breath starts tasting sour, or you vomit or faint, it is important to seek medical attention immediately.
A healthy heart is an essential part of your overall health. Wearables help you keep your heart healthy by providing features such as heart rate monitoring, alerts, and electrocardiograms.
Sleep is the body\'s natural way to recover physically and mentally. Pair a Huawei wearable to track your sleep and learn more about your sleeping habits.
Body weight and body fat percentage are important basic physical indicators. Pair a smart scale to get data and suggestions for improving your physique.
Excessive stress can cause physical and mental distress and affect long-term health. Wearable devices provide stress detection and relief services, helping you effectively manage stress.
Blood sugar management is extremely important for those with diabetes. Pair a supported device to easily manage test results and monitor your blood sugar level throughout the day.
Blood pressure tests can be very important for those with high blood pressure. Pair a supported device to manage your data and stay on top of your blood pressure.
SpO₂ is an important indicator of the respiratory system. Pair a device that supports SpO₂ monitoring to accurately record your SpO₂ level and get a notification when it\'s low.

Huawei Health is able to detect exercise intensity for a variety of different activities. For example, with a wearable it can detect when heart rate exceeds the maximum heart rate value (%1$s) or without a wearable it can detect when cadence exceeds %2$s, as well as if the user is cycling or following an exercise video, and mark these periods of moderate to high intensity activity. The app then uses an algorithm to calculate the total moderate to high intensity activity time.

Body temperature is a basic vital indicator. Pair a supported Huawei wearable to measure your body and wrist temperature. It can also alert you if your temperature is too high or low.

Body temperature is a person\'s internal temperature. It is important to maintain a constant body temperature for a stable metabolism and other physiological functions. Body temperature fluctuates and is usually lower in the morning and higher in the afternoon and evening. Body temperature should be %1$s–%2$s (armpit). A temperature below %3$s indicates hypothermia and %4$s or above indicates a fever.

Skin temperature is the temperature at the surface of a person\'s skin. Skin temperature varies across the body and can be affected by environmental and physical conditions. At typical room temperature, skin temperature at the wrist should be between %1$s and %2$s. Maintaining a comfortable temperature is important.
Results may be affected by the environment. These tips can help improve accuracy:
Make sure the strap is fastened securely and wear the device for at least %1$s minutes before measuring. Ideal room temperature is around %2$s. Keep still and relaxed during the measurement.
Make sure there isn\'t anything like water or alcohol on your wrist.
Avoid measuring in environments with direct sunlight, wind, or extreme temperatures.
The device won\'t output a result when it isn\'t being worn or the ambient temperature is below %1$s or above %2$s.
Results are for reference only and should not be used for medical diagnosis.
Wait for at least %s minutes after working out, showering, or switching between outside/inside before measuring.

The eating window can only be opened once per day. Wait for tomorrow to eat again.
Fasting for less than %d hours will affect fat-burning effects. Start early anyway?
Make sure to eat enough fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins. Are you sure you\'re ready to start fasting so soon?
During this period you might start to feel sleepy, which is normal. %1$d–%2$d minutes after eating, digestion starts causing blood sugar to stay relatively high for %3$d hours.
Your blood sugar will start decreasing as your body continues to consume glucose. You might start feeling hungry. Hold on for a little longer for your body to start burning stored fat.
During this period your body is consuming stored sugar, your blood sugar should remain stable. In a little while, you\'ll reach the fat-burning phase.
Fat burning phase (after fasting %d hours or more)
You\'ve reached the fat-burning phase. Your body is consuming fatty acids and turning them into ketones at an accelerated rate. Hang in there for a little longer for a better effect.

Avoid drinking strong tea, coffee, or other caffeinated drinks. Energy and caffeinated drinks are stimulants and can affect sleep quality.
Maintain a balanced diet. It\'s difficult to digest all your food if you eat a lot right before bed.
Try a hot bath before bed to increase blood circulation and clear your mind for a better night\'s sleep.
Relax before going to bed, composing yourself before bed can help you fall asleep.
Try not to think about anything too much before bed to avoid anxiety, nervousness, overstimulation, and other things that could prevent you from falling asleep.
Take a half-hour walk after dinner to help tire yourself out before bed.
Getting %1$d minutes of moderate to high-intensity activity during the day (such as jogging, swimming, or yoga) can help you fall asleep, but avoid high-intensity exercise %2$d hours before bed.
Reading a little before bed can also help you fall asleep faster.
Make sure the lighting isn\'t too bright where you are sleeping.
Avoid sleeping more than %1$d minutes during the day, otherwise it can be harder to fall asleep at night.
The ideal time to go to sleep is around %1$d PM.
Try to go to sleep and wake up at the same time every day to maintain your circadian cycle.
If you find it hard to fall asleep, try doing something to help you relax.
If you wake up too early in the morning, stay lying down with your eyes closed and wait for when you\'re supposed to get up.
It can be beneficial to stretch in the morning.
Try not to get worked up before bed, big mood swings can overstimulate your brain.
Try to take a half-hour nap during the day.
Not getting enough sleep can affect your focus. It\'s recommended to get at least %1$d hours of sleep each night.
Not getting enough sleep can affect how you interact with others and your productivity. It\'s recommended to get at least %1$d hours of sleep each night.
You can try using earplugs or earmuffs to block out noise.
Turn off the TV, speakers, and other devices that output sound and silence your phone before going to bed.
Looking at your phone too much before bed can stimulate your brain and make it harder to fall asleep or even cause insomnia. Try not to look at it too much before bed.

The blue light emitted by your phone screen can interrupt the production of melatonin, which helps regulate sleep. Constantly looking at your phone can overstimulate your brain and make it harder to fall asleep.
It can help to set rules for yourself, like stop using your phone half an hour before going to bed.
Try other activities like listening to music or a podcast instead of playing with your phone before going to sleep.
If you have a habit of smoking or drinking before bed, you can try reducing or cutting them out.
You can try sleeping on your right side to see if it reduces snoring.
Make sure you\'re using a suitable pillow. If it\'s too soft, it won\'t support your neck and could make your neck muscles tighter, which increases snoring.
Try not to think about too much before bed. Mood swings and overthinking stimulate your brain and can make it harder to fall asleep.
Try to clear your mind before falling asleep, otherwise you could have too many dreams.
If you usually have to get up during the night to go to the bathroom, you could try drinking less water during the day so you can sleep through the night.
You can supplement your diet with foods rich in vitamin B1, such as carrots, tomatoes, and grapes to help you fall asleep.
Waking up easily during the night could be due to a noisy sleeping environment. You can try sleeping with earplugs.
If you find yourself waking up too early, it\'s recommended to stay lying down with your eyes closed until it\'s time to get up.
Exercising every day and eating three meals a day can help your body get used to a routine and help regulate your sleeping schedule too.
A routine schedule can help your body detoxify and increase your immune system, reducing your chances of getting sick.
Sleeping too much can mess up your circadian cycle. It\'s ideal to stick to a schedule of sleeping %1$d–%2$d hours each night.
Setting an alarm can help you wake up at the same time every day.
Taking a %1$d-minute nap in the afternoon can help you refocus.
Taking some time during the day to rest and relax can increase your overall productivity.
Eating a diet rich in tryptophan, such as bananas, milk, and rice, can help your muscles relax and increase the production of melatonin, which will help you sleep better.
You are well rested. A half-hour jog can help you stay energized throughout the day.

The key to losing weight is that calorie intake is less than the amount of calories you burn off every day. A safe calorie deficit is between %1$s–%2$s (roughly %3$s a week).

Those with a higher initial body weight can consider increasing the deficit to about %1$s per day. An extreme calorie deficit will trigger a decrease in your basal metabolism as your body tries to protect itself, making it easier for your weight to rebound.

Huawei Health is able to detect exercise intensity for a variety of different activities. For example, with a wearable it can detect when heart rate exceeds the maximum heart rate value (%1$s) or without a wearable it can detect when cadence exceeds %2$s. If you don\'t have a Huawei or Honor device, it\'s recommended to pair a wearable for more accurate data, it can also detect if you are cycling or following an exercise video, and mark these periods of moderate to high intensity activity. The app then uses an algorithm to calculate the total moderate to high intensity activity time.

Exercise is the total duration of moderate- and high-intensity exercise you\'ve engaged in today. A longer duration indicates higher activity.

The World Health Organization recommends that adults engage in at least %1$d–%2$d minutes of moderate-intensity physical exercise, %3$d minutes of high-intensity physical exercise, or a combination of both every week.

Regular physical activity is good for your health. The Health app is capable of sensing a variety of activities and assessing the intensity, then deploying an algorithm to calculate exercise time. Completing fitness courses will also count towards exercise time. The latest Huawei wearables can start recording exercise time when your heart rate rises above a certain value or is higher than your walking heart rate for over %s. Non-Huawei users need to pair a wearable to get accurate data.

After your request to deactivate the service has been approved, unless otherwise specified by laws or regulations, all your data from Huawei Health (including, but not limited to, information about your account, authorized logins, and linked accounts) will be deleted or anonymized, after which it won\'t be possible to recover or retrieve it. To ensure the security of your account and assets, you must understand, agree with, and abide by the following terms and conditions to deactivate this service. Huawei Health reserves the right to require further verification.
The account is able to use Huawei Health services normally. The account isn\'t locked, stolen, or frozen, and doesn\'t have abnormal transactions or other risks.
%d. Huawei Health related benefits have been cleared or handled properly

Before deactivating the service, all Huawei Health benefits (such as member services, Guardian service, auto-renewal of subscriptions, paid resources, K-points, coupons, and credit-related benefits) need to be used up or cleared. If you deactivate the service without handling them, you voluntarily forfeit these benefits.

The service cannot be deactivated if you are a party to any ongoing disputes, such as if you are in the process of reporting another user or being reported yourself, or are involved in arbitration or litigation.

Deactivating this service will permanently delete all your Huawei Health data from the cloud and the device, including personal information and health and fitness data. Cloud syncing will be disabled, and achievements, medals, and K-points data will also be reset. Fitness data will no longer be shared to third-party apps unless you reenable \"Sync fitness data to cloud\".
%d. Manually log out of Huawei Health on all your devices

Once deactivated, Huawei Health services won\'t be available on other devices, such as a phone or tablet. You\'ll need to reactivate the service to use it again. If you stay logged in on other devices, the app may continue to produce personal data.

Disabling service will close Huawei Health, clear all personal data cached on the device, and stop the collection of user data from the device.\n\nDeactivating service will close Huawei Health, clear all personal data cached on the cloud and on the device, and stop the collection of user data from the device.

Your BMI is within the healthy range, but you have low skeletal muscle mass, this may cause your metabolism to slow down. You have too much visceral fat, this could develop into metabolic syndrome and other conditions in adulthood. Avoid high-fat foods. Try adding more strength training, especially for your back and core.

Your BMI is within the healthy range, but you have low skeletal muscle mass in your limbs, this may cause your metabolism to slow down and lower your athletic performance. Try sports, swimming, and other outdoor activities and proportionally increase your intake of high-quality protein.

Your BMI is within the healthy range, but you have too much visceral fat, if this continues past adolescence it could put you at a higher risk of developing high blood pressure, diabetes, high cholesterol, and other conditions. You also have low skeletal muscle mass, this may cause your metabolism to slow down and lower your athletic performance. Try to control your caloric intake and increase aerobic exercise.

You have a moderate amount of visceral fat, but your BMI is too high, increasing the risk of leg joint injury and joint deformation. You also have low skeletal muscle mass, this may cause your metabolism to slow down. Make appropriate adjustments to your diet and increase strength training, but avoid exercises that may put added stress on your leg joints, such as running and jumping rope.

Why do we need to collect your personal information?

We collect information about your sex and date of birth to provide suitable exercise recommendations. Your height and weight are used to calculate and increase the accuracy of various fitness and health metrics. If you decide not to provide this information or provide inaccurate information, our algorithm might not provide the best suggestions and metrics for you.
Why do we need to collect your emergency information?

Emergency information is used by Emergency SOS on your device for features, such as making an emergency call or viewing your medical information during an emergency. This information is never stored on our servers. If you decide not to provide this information, it won\'t impact other Health app features. You can check if your device supports Emergency SOS by seeing if a menu for it exists in Settings > Security & privacy.

Emergency information is used for Emergency SOS features on your device, such as making an emergency call or viewing your medical information during an emergency. This information is never stored on our servers. If you decide not to provide this information, it won\'t impact other Health app features.

SWOLF is a way of measuring your swimming efficiency. It is calculated per length (ideally in a %1$d%2$s swimming pool) by comparing the length of time it took you to swim the length with the number of strokes it took you to swim it. The smaller the value, the higher the efficiency.

Training at this level is still good for your general fitness, but will result in no obvious improvement of your aerobic capacity. Suitable for beginners, people getting back in to training, or for recovery days.
Training at this level will maintain your aerobic capacity, providing a solid foundation for higher intensity training.
Training at this level will enhance your aerobic capacity, making it a good intensity to aim for during regular training sessions.
Training at this level will greatly benefit your aerobic capacity, but requires a good balance of rest as well.
Training at this level is excessive and may result in injury. Be careful not to overtrain, and give yourself plenty of time to rest and recover.
Until you\'re ready for your next workout.
Focus on recovery. You can do low intensity activities, but wait until %s before returning to normal training.

Cycling cadence is an important piece of information when cycling. Cadence indicates how many full rotations are completed each minute. A full rotation means each foot pushes down on a pedal once – cadence is only calculated by counting the number of times one foot returns to the same point every minute. The unit is rpm, rotations per minute.
Cadence is measured as how many steps are taken every minute. This counts every time either foot steps down on the pedals. The unit is spm, steps per minute.

Elliptical cadence is also measured as how many full rotations are made every minute. A full rotation means each foot pushes down on a pedal once, two steps is equal to one rotation. The unit is rpm, rotations per minute.

Strokes is the cumulative number of full stroke movements. This varies by swimming stroke. For breaststroke and butterfly, both hands move together, so each time the arms move is a full movement and counts as a stroke. For freestyle and backstroke both hands move separately, so both arms need to enter the water once for it to count as one stroke.
Stroke rate refers to how many strokes are taken each minute. The unit is strokes per minute.
Safe to continue moderate intensity training

Heart rate recovery is how much your heart rate decreases at different times immediately after exercising. It is an important indicator of the body\'s condition, health, and overall aerobic performance. Wearing a heart rate monitor while running not only helps with training, but can also provide insights about your workout so you can adjust your training plan to improve heart rate recovery.

A good running route can help improve training efficiency and make exercising more exciting. Trying different routes can help you stick to the sport. This feature will help you find routes so you can keep running even when you\'re in an unfamiliar place.
The feature will detect your location and display routes near you. It will also display the total length of the route and total number of times Health users have completed the route.
You can tap the city name in the top left of the screen to view routes in other cities. Huawei Health will continue to add routes for more cities as they are verified.
Running routes are provided by Huawei Health and are based on big data, so runners can find quality routes no matter where they are. Making it easier to get out and exercise.

This is a combination of exercise and art. We\'re able to turn patterns into a route you can run along. The route will have required checkpoints. Hit all the checkpoints on your run to generate a piece of art. The thumbnail image may differ from the actual route and generated art.
%d. How can I share my RouteDraw art?

After completing a RouteDraw route, the system will generate a workout record. Go to Exercise records to view the actual route you ran and the image you drew. You can also choose to share your workout data or the art you created.
%d. What is the distance next to the route?
This is the total length of the route and is the minimum distance required to complete it.
%d. How does it determine if the route has been completed?

To follow a route, select it and tap Start workout. Try to follow the planned route as best as possible. Once the workout is finished, Huawei Health will compare the actual route you ran with the planned route. If there is at least a %s match, it will count as successful completion.

Currently this feature is available in multiple cities including Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou, Shenzhen, Nanjing, Wuhan, Chengdu, and Xi\'an. Huawei will continue to add cities as more routes are verified.

There are two different types of rankings: total number of times a user completed that route and time it took to complete the route. Huawei Health will automatically update your rankings based on your workout data. Only the top %d will be displayed. The top runner is the route MVP, and their profile pic will be shown on the route along with their anonymized nickname.
Download offline maps to use on your wearables.

Open Huawei Health and go to Exercise > Outdoor run/cycle to discover nearby routes. These are routes that are used frequently by outdoor sports enthusiasts and are provided to help improve training results and keep things fresh.
Popular routes are provided by Huawei Health based on big data, so outdoor sports enthusiasts can find quality routes in Huawei Health and improve their training results.
%d. How do I tell if I have completed my route?

To follow a route, select it and tap Start workout. Try to follow the planned route as best as possible. Once the workout is finished, Huawei Health will compare the actual route you took with the planned route. If there is at least a %s match, it will count as successful completion.

There are two different types of rankings: total number of times a user completed that route and their results for that route. Huawei Health will automatically update your rankings based on your workout data. Only the top %d will be displayed. The top user is the route MVP, and their profile pic will be shown on the route along with their anonymized nickname.\n\nNote: Suspicious completion of a route (for instance, beating the world record) won\'t be included in the rankings.

%1$d. Make sure to perform the test in an open area. It\'s best to find a well-marked spot as the starting point, make sure the direction you are jumping is clear too.

%1$d. Stand behind the starting line with your feet naturally apart. Then jump with both feet leaving the ground at the same time, your heels should touch the ground first, make sure to keep your knees bent and balanced. The distance from the starting point to where your heels first touched the ground is the distance you jumped.
%1$d. Place the device somewhere flat in a vertical position. Stand about %2$s away and face the screen head on. Make sure your whole body is in the frame and start from position 1.
%1$d. After the countdown, start jumping with both feet in the following sequence: 1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 1, repeat the cycle 10 times.

Data from your workouts will be synced between the Health app and workout machine and displayed in real time. This data includes duration, distance, speed, pace, heart rate, calories, and step count. Workout data provided by the machine may be less accurate than that provided by your phone. Personal information, such as your nickname, age, sex, height, and weight, will not be sent to the machine. The workout machine may store the aforementioned workout data and upload it to third-party servers. You are solely responsible for exercising your rights with regard to data stored on the machine or third-party servers, which may include having this data amended, deleted, or restricted. It is your responsibility to ensure that your machine has been certified or authorized by Huawei, and to make sure that you stop using the machine before using a phone, tablet, or wearable device. You understand that you shall bear all risks, including but not limited to personal injury or the disclosure of your personal information, that may result from the misuse of this machine or the use of an unauthorized machine.

Your level is a measure of your experience in Huawei Health. The more experience you have, the higher your level.
How is my new level calculated?

Your new level is calculated by converting the number of days that you completed your fitness goal into experience points. Your new level won\'t be lower than the original one. The more days you completed your goal, the higher your new level will be.
You can earn experience points by reaching daily fitness milestones, such as walking a certain number of steps, going out for a run, or completing a workout.
The steps-for-points rules have been adjusted as follows:
If you walk more than 10,000 steps in a day, you\'ll earn 20 experience points. If you complete under 10,000 steps, you\'ll earn a proportional amount of points.
New privileges and more ways to earn experience points are coming soon.

K-points are points you earn by being active in the Health app and can be exchanged for chances at the lucky draw or for %s.
You can earn K-points by going to Me then touching K-points under your name and completing any of the tasks listed.

The calories you burn during recorded activity are converted into K-points for you to claim. For every %1$d Calories burned, you earn %2$d K-point (%3$d kcal = %4$d K-point). For privacy reasons, only data synced to the cloud can be counted.
Only the first %1$d Calories you burn each day can be converted into K-points, so you can only earn up to %2$d K-points a day through burning calories.

The calories you burn can only be converted into K-points that day, calories will not roll over to the next day. Your calories will be converted as you burn them throughout the day, but they can only be converted once. The total exchanged each day can be viewed in your K-point details.

Calories burned through daily activity will be automatically converted into K-points. To claim them, just go to Me and touch K-points under your name. You can claim them multiple times throughout the day. You need to have burned at least %1$d kcal to get a K-point. If you haven\'t burned another %2$d kcal since the last time you claimed K-points, you won\'t be able to claim any. If you were unable to claim K-points, you can still try to claim them later that day.

K-points are points you earn by logging in and being active in the Health app. You can get them by cashing in your daily calories and completing tasks. K-points can be exchanged for Vmall Points. You can download the Vmall app or visit the website (%s) and log in to your HUAWEI ID to use the Points for discounts, redeem prizes, participate in lucky draws, and more.

The new system has %1$d different icons, with %2$d different levels, including Budding health star, Self-discipline expert, Health guru, Ace, Elite, Legend, and Peak athlete. The icons are to record your growth from using the Health app and will change as you go.

The %1$d new icons are based on the original %2$d levels. The experience points required for each icon are as follows:
Budding health star: %3$d–%4$d
Self-discipline expert: ≤%5$d–%6$d
Health guru: ≤%7$d–%8$d
Ace: ≤%9$d–%10$d
Elite: ≤%11$d–%12$d
Legend: ≤%13$d–%14$d
Peak athlete: ≤%15$d

(%1$d) You can earn points by completing daily tasks. Each task is worth a certain number of experience points. For example: weighing yourself or running %2$d km. You can check the level screen for daily tasks so you can earn more points.\n(%3$d) Your daily step count will automatically be converted into a certain number of points.

Data on your total number of points is saved on the cloud. If the number doesn't seem right, it may be because:
(%1$d) The Internet connection is unstable. Check your network connection, refresh the page and look again.
(%2$d) You didn't complete an actual task. Not everything results in earning experience points.
(%3$d) There are certain measures in place to prevent users from earning points unfairly, if points are found to have been earned using cheating, tricking the system, attacking the network, or other alternative methods, points will not be awarded.

Personalized tags are based on your use of the Health app, users can choose which can be seen. These tags will change as you continue your journey. These are simply based on your data and are not meant to reflect your actual person.
You can check the percentage of users you exceeded on the level screen. This ranking is based on your current experience points total.
Once you activate a membership, the number of points you earn will be multiplied by %1$s, so you can upgrade faster.

Personal information, including HUAWEI ID account info (such as age and sex), height, and weight.

Fitness data, including device location, workout route, type, and duration, running form, step count, distance covered, heart rate, calories burned, ascent, VO2Max, and other workout and activity metrics.

Health data, including sleep, heart rate, heart rate variability, stress level, ECG, pulse wave, arterial stiffness, arrhythmia analysis, skin temperature, blood oxygen (SpO2), blood pressure, respiratory check, menstrual cycle, emotions (neutral, pleasant, unpleasant), weight, weight management, and body composition, as well as diet-related data and metrics. Various health-related trends and insights are generated through individual analysis or by correlating different types of health data.

Device and network information, including device identifiers, types, and settings, personal settings, network type, and list of installed map apps. Device identifier is used for identifying and managing your devices.

Your personal, health, and fitness data includes sensitive personal data. The processed data depends on the services you are using, the capabilities of your wearable, and the data you input yourself. Your wearable syncs data to Huawei Health only after you have authorized pairing. Sensitive data is stored by default on your device and only uploaded to our servers with your explicit consent. The servers are located in %s.

The synced data depends on the services you are using, the capabilities of your wearable, and the data you actively submit.\nThe cloud option provides a secure environment for storing and syncing your data across devices, and allows for deeper analysis of your fitness and health journey through trends and insights derived from various data points over time.

Health data, including sleep, sleep breathing, heart rate, heart rate variability, stress, ECG, arterial stiffness, blood oxygen, blood pressure, emotions (pleasant, neutral, and unpleasant), respiratory check, high altitude evaluations, weight, weight management, and body composition, as well as diet-related data and metrics.

Fitness data, including device location, workout route, type, duration, and heart rate, step count, wheelchair push count and frequency, group rankings, distance, calories burned, ascent, VO2Max, and other workout and activity analysis data.\nSome fitness data may reveal aspects of your health, such as exercise heart rate and certain activity types, for example, wheelchair-based activities.

Ensure adequate amounts of sleep every day for the maintenance of physical and mental health. The United States National Sleep Foundation suggests that adults sleep between %1$d-%2$d hours.

Duration of sleep is not the only criterion that determines sleep quality, as demonstrated by the presence of long sleepers (≥ %1$s) and short sleepers (≤ %2$s) in the population. Sleeping less than the recommended amount should not be a cause for anxiety, as long as you wake up feeling refreshed. In addition, sleeping for a prolonged time does not necessarily make up for a lack of sleep and may negatively impact sleep quality.

Normal sleep architecture includes REM (rapid eye movement) and NREM (non-rapid eye movement) sleep. NREM sleep includes light and deep sleep, and REM sleep is usually associated with dreaming. REM sleep and NREM sleep alternate in a cycle consisting of one period of REM and one of NREM. There are usually %1$d–%2$d cycles during the night. Generally each REM-NREM cycle lasts %3$d–%4$d minutes, and the REM portion increases each time.

A typical sleep cycle includes these stages: Awakening → light sleep → deep sleep → light sleep → REM. However, you might not experience all these stages, and the order in which they occur is not fixed. For example, a person can transition directly from light sleep, deep sleep, or REM to awakening.

Inadequate sleep at night can be supplemented by a nap during the day. In general, a nap before 1 pm lasting %1$d-%2$d minutes allows you to re-energize, improving clarity and efficiency in the afternoon.

Keep naps shorter than %1$s to avoid deep sleep. If you sleep too deeply, your body temperature will drop, and you might wake up feeling sleepy, dizzy, and more tired. Deep sleep during the daytime will also impact the quality of sleep you get at night.
* Sleeping for less than %1$s at a time will be counted as a nap.

The most important part of the biological clock is the body temperature rhythm. When body temperature rises, you will feel more awake and when body temperature drops, you will feel tired. Temperatures fluctuate periodically throughout the day, rising in the morning, sometimes going down in the afternoon, and then rising until night. Therefore, it is best to nap in the afternoon (usually before 1 pm) when your body temperature is low, as the effect is best. If you nap too late in the day, the nap will interfere with your body temperature rhythm, affecting quality of sleep at night.

You may be waking at night for a number of reasons, including insomnia, sleep deprivation, and oversleeping. Under normal circumstances, you should wake up fewer than %1$s per night, and the total amount of time you spend awake each night should be less than %2$s of total sleep time. If you wake up frequently in the night, you may have insomnia. Research shows that individuals with insomnia spend significantly less time in deep sleep than normal sleepers. Associated symptoms include low energy levels, even with normal amounts of sleep time.
%1$d. How can I prevent waking up often during the night?

If you\'re experiencing difficulty sleeping on an ongoing basis, you may be able to shed more light on the issue by looking at sleep study research done by the Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School.

(%1$d) Develop regular lifestyle habits and strengthen the body\'s circadian rhythm. Go to bed on time, and get up on time the next day even if you could not sleep at night. Receive adequate sunlight during the day, and avoid naps that are too long.

(%1$d) Avoid activities that require intense brain activity before going to bed. For example, while you are in bed, do not watch TV, use your mobile phone, or go over problems. Go to bed relaxed and in a calm emotional state.
(%1$d) Develop a correct understanding of sleep and do not enlarge the consequences of poor sleep. If you wake up at night, do not get overly anxious, do not frequently look at the time, and relax.
(%1$d) The bedroom environment should be comfortable, with suitable temperatures, and without noise and light.
(%1$d) Maintain regular, moderate exercise (such as jogging). Exercise can promote sleep at night, but remember to avoid intense exercise before going to bed.

When the body is fatigued, or when an illness affects the upper airway (such as colds, nasal congestion, sore throat, etc.), there will be excessive airway stenosis, resulting in snoring, low ventilation, and sleep apnea, affecting breathing quality during sleep. In addition, weight gain also affects sleep quality.

For healthy individuals, if the number of irregular respirations is less than %1$s per hour, then sleep quality is usually above %2$s. If the number of irregular respirations is more than %3$s, then sleep quality is usually below %4$s.

If you notice yourself sleeping poorly with irregular breathing when you are tired or after drinking alcohol, then you probably don\'t need to worry about treatment. If you often have trouble breathing, sleep on your side and avoid smoking or drinking alcohol. Exercise frequently to improve sleep quality. If you notice yourself breathing poorly during sleep for an extended period of time, and the symptoms below appear, please seek medical advice.
(%1$d) Waking in the night, or increased nocturnal urination
(%1$d) Sleep is not refreshing, wake up feeling fatigued
(%1$d) Dry mouth in the early morning, acid reflux, heartburn, or similar symptoms
(%1$d) Loud, uneven snoring and sleep apnea

In the light sleep stage, brain wave activity, heart rate, and breathing rate all slow down. In this sleep stage, it is easy to wake up. Being able to wake from sleep is a defense mechanism, essential to health and survival, and light sleep is a normal physiological need. Too much light sleep, though, can damage sleep quality and cause fatigue and difficulty waking.
%1$d. How can I avoid getting too much light sleep?

Cognitive behavioral therapy is recommended by the American College of Physicians (ACP) as the initial treatment for chronic insomnia in adults. Clinical practice shows that chronic insomniacs often have room for improvement in their sleeping habits. The Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School, provides the following tips on improving sleep quality and getting more deep sleep:
(%1$d) Psychological factors: Excess energy, anxiety, mental tension, and depression can all disturb deep sleep. Balanced moods promote deep sleep and aid sleep quality.
(%1$d) Physiological factors: Excessive fatigue, or conditions with uncomfortable symptoms such as pain, itching and coughing, can all disturb deep sleep.

(%1$d) Environmental factors: Light, noise, and temperature and humidity levels can all disturb deep sleep. Creating a quiet, comfortable environment and choosing suitable bedding can help you sleep deeply.

(%1$d) Schedule: Shifts in work schedule, jet lag, or an irregular work/rest schedule can all disturb your biological clock and prevent you getting enough deep sleep. A regular sleep schedule helps your body clock work naturally and improves sleep quality.

(%1$d) Diet: Caffeine, alcohol, nicotine, and stimulating food can cause you to sleep too lightly. Following a balanced diet, avoiding caffeine after midday, and moderating alcohol and tobacco promotes deep sleep.

(%1$d) Exercise: A suitable exercise schedule encourages deep sleep, as the body increases its rate of deep sleep in order to recover from the exercise. Strenuous exercise should be avoided close to bedtime, as it may make it difficult to fall asleep.

Brain wave frequency, breathing rate, and blood pressure are reduced during deep sleep. Deep sleep, also known as slow-wave sleep, is the deepest sleep stage and the hardest to wake from. Being woken during this stage may result in dizziness, palpitations, and irritability. Research shows that deep sleep is most effective for eliminating fatigue. Deep sleep is essential for stabilizing mood, balancing the mind, and restoring energy. Usually, more deep sleep means better quality sleep, but very high levels of deep sleep over a long period may be a symptom of illness. If in doubt, contact a medical professional.
%1$d. How can I get enough deep sleep?
%1$d. What is rapid eye movement (REM) sleep?

In the REM sleep phase, the human eyes rapidly move back and forth under the eyelids. Most people woken during this sleep stage say they are dreaming. During REM sleep, it is harder to wake than during light sleep, but easier than during deep sleep. Muscles also become relaxed; this is a safety mechanism to prevent acting out in dreams. This explains why you feel like you can\'t run and shout in dreams.
%1$d. How can I have healthy REM sleep?

Healthy REM sleep helps maintain mental well-being, boosts creativity, and relieves stress. Studies show that subjects are more likely to experience irritability, tension, and fatigue when deprived of REM sleep. Dreams mostly occur during REM sleep, and too many dreams can indicate that REM sleep accounts for too much of overall sleep. Dreaming often does not necessarily indicate poor sleep quality, rather it indicates that sleep was not deep enough, which results in easily-remembered dreams.

When REM levels are low, the body automatically catches up within a few nights. If you have excessive or insufficient levels of REM sleep over a long period, contact a medical professional, especially if you are also experiencing irritability, anxiety, or similar symptoms.
The Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School, provides the following tips on getting the right amount of REM sleep:
(%1$d) Schedule: REM sleep usually lasts longer in the second half of the night. Maintaining a regular schedule by waking up and going to bed early can help.
(%1$d) Psychological factors: Avoiding extreme anxiety or fatigue and handling daily stressors efficiently can help to maintain normal levels of REM sleep.
(%1$d) Diet: Caffeine, alcohol, nicotine, and medication can negatively impact the amount of REM sleep you get, resulting in REM sleep accounting for too little of overall sleep.
Prevalent Hypertension and Stroke in the Sleep Heart Health Study: Association with an ECG-derived Spectrographic Marker of Cardiopulmonary Coupling. Sleep, 2009, 32(7): 897-904.

* The Center for Dynamical Biomarkers: the Center for Dynamical Biomarkers, BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School
Note: HUAWEI TruSleep™ is a sleep monitoring technology certified by the Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School.
*This is not a medical device, sleep data and recommendations are provided for reference only.

Normal sleep architecture includes REM (rapid eye movement) and NREM (non-rapid eye movement) sleep. NREM sleep includes light sleep and deep sleep, and REM sleep is usually associated with dreaming. Huawei/Honor wearable devices are equipped with HUAWEI TruSleep™ sleep monitoring technology, which extracts normal sinus rhythm and respiratory signals from heart rate signals and uses the Hilbert-Huang transform (HHT) technology to analyze the coherence of these signals and their cross-spectral power for cardiopulmonary coupling analysis. This allows for accurate analysis for various stages of sleep, including deep sleep, light sleep, REM sleep, and waking.

The sleep score is used mainly to assess sleep quality. It is based on multiple parameters in the sleep structure, such as total sleep time and ratio of time for each sleep stage. Among these factors, deep sleep is a significant factor. If deep sleep is too short or fragmented, the brain cannot get adequate rest, which means even if the overall sleep time is long, you will still feel drowsy and a lack of energy upon waking up. The recommended times for each sleep stage are for healthy adults and are based on the statistical results of the Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School, using CPC technology to analyze healthy adults in the SHHS database.
%1$d. How can sleep quality be improved?

Poor quality sleep is usually the result of poor sleeping habits, such as an irregular schedule, lack of sun exposure, lack of exercise, regular caffeine intake, drinking, or smoking. Sleep quality is also affected by psychological, physiological, and environmental factors, such as anxiety, fatigue, bedroom lighting, noise, temperature and humidity.

Understanding the science behind sleep, analyzing and finding poor sleeping habits and addressing the various factors that negatively impact sleep, and making timely adjustments can improve sleep quality. Huawei/Honor wearable devices scientifically assess your sleep quality and give you suggestions according to a methodology recognized by the Center for Dynamical Biomarkers (DBIOM) at BIDMC, a major teaching hospital of Harvard Medical School.

Huawei Vmall is an app that provides services such as QR code login, product browsing, online purchasing and pre-ordering, product delivery, and service center locating. To provide the above services, this app needs to connect to the Internet during use, and requires the storage, Phone, Camera, and location permissions. The following information will be collected and used:
Device information, including device identifier, product model, operating system, and app version.
Network information, including IP address and GPS location.

HUAWEI ID information, including username, nickname, date of birth, delivery address, name, phone number, postal code, email address, ID information, bank card number, membership status and privileges, subscription and purchase records.
Operation records, including browsing records and user touches.
Other information you submit, including comments, issues, and suggestions.
The above data will be sent to and stored on servers in the People\'s Republic of China.
Even after exiting, this app may still connect to the Internet in the background to provide certain services, such as updating and notification receipt.

This app will only work if you agree to the collection of the above information and grant the required permissions. You can uninstall or exit Huawei Vmall to stop the collection and processing of the above information.

%1$s %2$s: Advanced anaerobic interval. Running at this interval is exhausting, and it may be difficult to complete the workout. This is common during high-intensity interval training. This training can improve speed and anaerobic capacity.

%1$s %2$s: Basic anaerobic interval. Running at this level is exhausting. You may experience heavy breathing and be short of breath afterwards. This is common during moderate interval training. This training can exercise your lactic acid tolerance and anaerobic endurance.

%1$s %2$s: Lactic acid interval. Running at this level can be tiring, but you should be able to finish your workout. You may feel short of breath, and your muscles will feel a little heavy from the accumulation of lactic acid. This can train your lactic acid threshold, and your body\'s ability to drain the acid. This will also enhance your aerobic ability.

%1$s %2$s: Advanced aerobic interval. Running at this interval can be a little hard. You may experience heavier breathing but not be short of breath. This interval is good for distance running. Training at this intensity, in addition to improving aerobic endurance, is also ideal for adjusting your marathon pace.

%1$s %2$s: Basic aerobic interval. Running at this interval is easy. Breathing should be natural, and you should be able to hold a conversation. Training at this interval can increase your cardiac muscle strength, number of blood vessels, and body\'s oxygen capacity. This will improve your aerobic endurance.

Your resting heart rate refers to the number of times your heart beats per minute while you\'re at rest. It is an important indicator of cardiovascular health, and is best measured just after waking up in the morning. The ideal resting heart rate is between %1$d-%2$d bpm. Individuals with resting heart rates outside of this range have a higher probability of developing cardiovascular diseases. You can improve your resting heart rate by exercising regularly.

Cadence refers to the number of steps taken per minute while running. A common target for running cadence is %1$d steps/minute. Maintaining a good cadence can help improve running efficiency and reduce the landing impact. Cadence can be increased through targeted training, such as sprinting with short strides or running with high knees.

Step length refers to the distance between a runner\'s feet when they successively hit the ground. Step length depends mainly on the runner\'s body shape, physical fitness, and technical skills. Step length can be improved through targeted training, such as long stride running or front kick exercises.

Ground contact time is the period of time your foot remains on the ground during your stride. A lower ground contact time usually coincides with better use of muscle elastic energy and running efficiency. For elite runners, the average ground contact time is less than %1$d ms. Ground contact time can be reduced through training exercises like jumping rope and squat jumps.

Landing impact refers to the impact shock at initial contact with the ground. One of the leading causes of injury is improper landing. This metric is measured by impact acceleration, and is usually affected by running speed. Higher speeds generally lead to harder impacts. The common range is %1$d-%2$d g (g is the gravitational acceleration). Through targeted training like vertical jumps and air squats, landing technique can be improved.

Foot strike pattern refers to how your foot initially touches the ground. It can be classified into forefoot strike, midfoot strike, and heel strike. Usually, it is better to land softly on the forefoot or the lateral forefoot to absorb the impact, then make full contact with the ground and push off quickly. This movement can be improved by running barefoot, or by doing exercises such as walking calf raises and half squat jumps.

Swing angle refers to the total amount the leg swings from push-off to the highest position as the lower leg moves up towards the thigh. A larger swing angle means a smaller motion radius, contributing to increased leg swing efficiency. This index usually increases with speed, and the common range is %1$d-%2$d°. Swing angle can be increased with targeted training such as back kicks and hip bridge exercises.

Eversion excursion refers to the inward rotation of the foot between touchdown and push-off. The average rotation angle is between %1$d-%2$d°. Abnormally large or small rotation angles may increase injury risks for runners. Ankle stability can be improved by doing calf raises and ankle circle exercises to reduce the risk of injury.

Maximum heart rate is the peak heart rate an athlete reaches when exercising. It can be used as a warning that the athlete is at their limit, as well as provide a measurable range for heart rate zones.
Anaerobic training effect refers to the benefits gained from training at a high intensity. High-intensity interval training can yield greater effects.
Aerobic training effect refers to the benefits gained by doing aerobic exercises. Increasing the length of aerobic training sessions can yield greater effects.

Recovery time refers to how long your body takes to fully recover and be ready for your next workout. By resting and having lighter training days, your body can better replenish itself and training will be more effective.

As your foot leaves the ground, bend your leg. You can also improve your swing angle through high knee running and glute bridges.
A swing angle in the normal range helps you run efficiently. To improve further, you can try lower-body strength training.

Swing angle naturally increases with speed. However, a large swing angle can also be caused by excessive calf movement, which is a waste of energy. Instead of kicking your heel up, move your knee forwards while folding your leg in.
Top athletes have a ground contact time of %1$d ms or under. Improve your ground contact time even further with exercises that increase leg muscle elasticity, such as skipping and jump squats.

Try to ensure your foot lands close to your body\'s center of gravity, and leaves the ground quickly after landing. Consider improving your lower-body strength through skipping, lunges, or jump squats.
A small eversion excursion carries a risk of ankle sprains. Try ankle circles and heel raises to improve the robustness of the ankle joints, and choose cushioned shoes to support your feet.
An eversion excursion in the normal range helps you absorb impact.
If you want to improve your ability to absorb impact, try lower-body strength training.

Harder impact risks damaging the knees and ankles. Try to land gently with your knee slightly bent and your foot close to your body\'s center of gravity. You may also benefit from lower-body strength exercises, such as air squats and glute bridges.

Landing on the forefoot helps cushion the impact of landing. Don\'t forget to exercise your calf muscles regularly and stretch after training, as this reduces the risk of injuring your Achilles tendon and plantar fascia.

Aim to land on the outside of your forefoot with your knee slightly bent. You can improve your foot strike with barefoot running, while deep squats and leg raises can help improve your lower-body strength.

Landing on the heel often creates a high landing impact. When running fast or sprinting, try to ensure your forefoot bears most of the landing impact. Barefoot running and high knee walking may help improve your foot strike, and lower-body strength exercises are also recommended.

A large eversion excursion increases the risk of spraining your ankle. Try ankle circles and heel raises to improve the robustness of the ankle joints, and choose stability shoes to support your feet.

Heart rate zone = Heart rate reserve x Heart rate zone percentage range + Resting heart rate. For example, John, aged %1$d, has a maximum heart rate of %2$d bpm and resting heart rate of %3$d bpm. His heart rate zone for aerobic improvement is:\n(%2$d - %3$d) x HR zone percentage range + %3$d = %4$d–%5$d bpm.

During deep sleep, the frequency of brain waves lowers significantly, as does blood pressure and the respiratory rate, which is why deep sleep is also called slow-wave sleep. The benefits of deep sleep depend to a large extent upon the quality of the sleep – in other words, whether it is continuous or fragmented. Studies have shown that only with a sufficient amount of deep sleep can the brain and body function properly, and the higher the level of deep sleep continuity, the more effective the rest.
%1$d. How can I increase deep sleep continuity?

According to the American College of Physicians\' (ACP) guidelines for the management of chronic insomnia in adults, cognitive and behavioral therapy are recommended as initial treatments for insomnia. Clinical practice has shown that people with chronic insomnia often have bad sleep habits. If you want to improve your sleep quality and increase the amount of continuous deep sleep you\'re getting, consult the following recommendations from the Harvard Medical School CDB Center:
(%1$d) Psychological factors: Excess energy, anxiety, mental tension, and depression can all lead to poor sleep continuity. Addressing these factors can promote deep sleep and aid sleep quality.
(%1$d) Physiological factors: Excessive fatigue, or conditions with uncomfortable symptoms such as pain, itching and coughing, can all disturb deep sleep continuity.

(%1$d) Environmental factors: Light, noise, temperature, and humidity levels can all disturb deep sleep. Creating a quiet, comfortable environment to sleep in and choosing suitable bedding can help you improve deep sleep continuity.

(%1$d) Schedule: Shifts in work schedule, jet lag, or an irregular work/rest schedule can all disturb your biological clock and disrupt your deep sleep continuity. A regular sleep schedule helps your body clock work naturally and improves sleep quality. Maintaining a regular schedule by going to bed and waking up at the same time every day helps to establish a natural circadian rhythm and can improve the continuity of your deep sleep.

(%1$d) Diet: Caffeine, nicotine, alcohol, and stimulating foods have a negative impact on sleep and lead to reduced deep sleep continuity. Foods rich in magnesium (such as leafy greens, avocados, bananas, and nuts) can help increase sleep quality and deep sleep continuity.

(%1$d) Exercise: An appropriate amount of physical exercise will increase fatigue to a level at which the body will naturally compensate by increasing the amount and continuity of the deep sleep it is getting. However, you should avoid strenuous exercise just before going to bed, as this can make going to sleep more difficult.

Blood oxygen saturation (SpO₂) is the percentage of hemoglobin in the blood that contains oxygen - in other words, the amount of oxygen in the blood. It is a useful natural indicator of the condition of a person\'s respiratory system. If your SpO₂ is below %1$s, you may be at risk of developing hypoxemia. Generally speaking, the lower your SpO₂, the higher the risk. Use the data below for reference:
2. Things to note when measuring SpO₂
%1$s. Enable SpO₂ on the wearable device. Follow the instructions for accurate results.

%1$s. Certain external factors may interfere with the measurement, causing it to be inaccurate or even fail. These include low blood perfusion, arm hair, tattoos, incorrect posture, moving your arm during the measurement, not wearing your device correctly, room temperature, and so on.
%1$s. Measurement results are for reference only and are not intended as a basis for medical diagnosis.

If you regularly wake up in the middle of the night and find it difficult to get back to sleep, and feel fatigued, anxious, or easily irritated as a result, see the following recommendations:

According to the American College of Physicians\' (ACP) guidelines for the management of chronic insomnia in adults, cognitive and behavioral therapy are recommended as initial treatments for insomnia. Clinical practice has shown that people with chronic insomnia often have bad sleep habits. If you want to improve your sleep quality and increase the amount of deep sleep you are getting, consult the following sleep improvement recommendations:
See the following recommendations for getting the right amount of REM sleep:

According to the American College of Physicians\' (ACP) guidelines for the management of chronic insomnia in adults, cognitive and behavioral therapy are recommended as initial treatments for insomnia. Clinical practice has shown that people with chronic insomnia often have bad sleep habits. If you want to improve your sleep quality and increase the amount of continuous deep sleep you are getting, consult the following sleep improvement recommendations:

Sleep quality index measures how well you rest. It is calculated by looking at a number of parameters including the total amount of sleep and the relative amounts of different sleep stages. Sufficient sleep is a biological necessity, and each and every sleep stage is essential for keeping physically and mentally sharp. The recommended sleep ranges for healthy adults are based on statistical analysis, and provide a scientific reference for improving sleep quality.

Understanding the science behind sleep, identifying the psychological, physiological, and environmental factors that negatively impact it, and then analyzing your own sleep habits can help you to make timely adjustments for better sleep. Huawei wearable devices scientifically assess your sleep quality and give you suggestions for how to improve it.

HUAWEI TruSleep™ monitors your sleep patterns, and can accurately detect if you are in a state of deep sleep, light sleep, REM sleep, or awake. It will evaluate the quality of the sleep you are getting and give you tips for how you can improve it.

Your vertical jump height is the vertical distance your device travels from the bottom to the top of your jump. This works best when the device is attached to the foot you usually use to push off with, and is affected by how you hold your leg when you\'re in the air (like straight or kicked up).
Jumping requires explosive power and coordination. Improve your vertical height with exercises like squats, static lunges, double-leg hops, and high knees.
Some professional athletes have a vertical jump height of %1$d cm.

Hang time is the duration of time from when your feet leave the ground to when you land. Attach the device to the foot you usually push off with for better results. Accuracy may vary depending on which foot lands first.
Hang time is influenced by your lower body and core strength. Improve your time with strength and core training including squats, V sit ups, plank with reaches, and V sits.
Professional athletes can sometimes reach %1$d ms of hang time.

Swing tempo is the ratio of time for your backswing to downswing. Professional golfers usually have a %1$d:%2$d swing tempo, but everyone swings at their own tempo. Practice to get a consistent swing tempo.
Swing speed is the speed of the club head when it hits the ball.
Backswing is the time from when you start the swing, swinging the club backwards off the ground to the top of the swing.
Downswing is the time from the top of the swing to when the club is swung back down to hit the ball.

Improve your flight time with deep squats, single-leg jumps, lunge jumps, and other exercises to increase the muscles in your legs as well as flexibility.

Flight time is measured as the time when both feet are in the air, not in contact with the ground. Generally speaking, under the same cadence, runners who have a longer flight time have a longer stride and better running efficiency. Professional runners\' flight times average about %1$d ms. Increase flight time with deep squats, single-leg jumps, lunge jumps, and other exercises.
Improve your ground contact time with deep squats, lunge jumps, jumping rope, and other exercises.

The ratio between ground contact and flight time. Under the same cadence, a shorter ground contact time indicates higher running efficiency. Professional runners\' flight ratios average about %1$s. Improve flight ratio with deep squats, lunge jumps, jumping rope, and other exercises.

Blood oxygen saturation (SpO₂) is the percentage of hemoglobin in the blood that contains oxygen - in other words, the amount of oxygen in the blood. It is a useful natural indicator of the condition of a person\'s respiratory system. The ideal range is between %1$s and %2$s, but this can be affected by your physical condition, altitude, and certain illnesses. If your SpO₂ level is too low it could indicate a health risk. Use the data below for reference:

Pace zones are calculated based on your actual running times and can be used to help with training.

Jog – in this zone you should be able to hold a conversation and maintain pace for a very long time. Running in this range is good for exercising your heart, improving basic aerobic endurance, and burning fat.

Marathon – in this zone you should be taking deep breaths without feeling short of breath and be able to maintain pace for a long period of time. Running in this zone is good for improving aerobic endurance and your pace, as well as practicing for marathons.

Lactate threshold – in this zone you should be starting to feel short of breath, but still able to maintain pace for a period of time. Running in this range can improve your lactate threshold and your ability to drain lactic acid. This can be achieved through tempo or interval runs. The ratio for running to rest for intervals should be %1$d:%2$d.

Anaerobic – this zone should be hard to maintain. When you\'re finished, you should be short of breath and unable to start talking immediately. Running in this zone is good for improving lactate threshold and anaerobic endurance.

High intensity – this zone should be the hardest and should push you to your limits. Running in this zone is good for training your mental strength and elicit your VO2Max. This is usually achieved through interval training. Your running to rest ratio should be %1$d:%2$d.

Recovery time is the amount of time required for your body to return to a fully recovered state after a workout. The time can be used as a reference for how intense your workouts should be. As the time gets shorter, you can consider increasing the intensity of your workouts.
Aerobic training stress refers to the stress put on your aerobic energy system during moderate to high intensity activity. Long-term, routine aerobic workouts can improve your aerobic capacity.
Anaerobic training stress refers to the stress put on your anaerobic energy system during high intensity activity. High intensity workouts can improve your speed.
%1$s–%2$s: Maintained – Comfortable for maintaining aerobic capacity.
%1$s–%2$s: Improved – Good for improving aerobic capacity.
%1$s: Excessive – This level can be dangerous. Continue with caution.
%1$s–%2$s: Maintained – Comfortable for maintaining anaerobic capacity.
%1$s–%2$s: Improved – Good for improving anaerobic capacity.

A proper diet can help improve your sleep. Foods rich in magnesium, such as green vegetables, avocados, bananas, and nuts, can improve sleep quality and continuity.

Your brain actively blocks the nerve channels for sound transmission during deep sleep, but too much noise can still interrupt your sleep. Closing your bedroom door and windows or using earplugs can reduce the impact of noise on your sleep.
Calming bedtime activities such as reading or listening to music can improve sleep.

Drinking too much water before bed can wake you up in the middle of the night. Instead, rehydrate with a glass of water in the morning, which can improve blood circulation and help you feel alert throughout the day.
Deep breathing exercises can help calm you and avoid overthinking before bed. With practice, these exercises will improve the quality of your deep sleep.

Is your mattress suitable for you? Lie down on the bed and try to insert your palm under your body. If it is difficult to do so, then your mattress is too soft. If your palm can move around easily, then the mattress is too firm. If your palm can fit perfectly beneath your waist, then your mattress provides the ideal support for your back.

A pillow firmness that suits your sleeping habits can help you fall asleep. Use a firmer pillow if you like to sleep on your side or back and a softer pillow if you like to sleep on your stomach. Adding herbs such as lavender, orange peel, or clover can also promote sleep.
Aromatherapy can help improve sleep quality. You can add lavender oil to your body wash or moisturizer or place a dry cloth on your forehead after soaking it in warm water with added lavender oil.

Caffeine, alcohol, and nicotine can make it more difficult to get enough sleep. These substances stimulate the central nervous system, which can lead to nocturnal awakenings. Replacing these with herbal drinks such as mint, chamomile, or rosehip tea can improve sleep quality.

Moderate exercise (such as speed walking and jogging for half an hour or more) can help improve the quality of your sleep. You can use the Huawei Health app to plan the intensity and length of your training.
